summary: Conjecture 4 of arXiv:2603.02786 asserts the precise asymptotic $M_{\mathbb{P}(n)}(n)\sim\frac{1}{6}\cdot\frac{n^{3}}{\ln n}$, where $\mathbb{P}(n)$ denotes the set of primes up to $n$ and $M_{\mathbb{P}(n)}(n)$ is the minimum interval length needed to pack the corresponding arithmetic progressions. The authors establish the matching lower bound (Theorem 5); the upper bound remains open. No follow-up resolving the conjecture was found in the two months since posting.
search_queries: packing arithmetic progressions Alon Debski Grytczuk Przybyo 2603.02786 conjecture asymptotic primes
packing arithmetic progressions primes asymptotic n^3/ln n conjecture 2026
arxiv 2603.02786 cite packing arithmetic progressions Alon 2026
notes: The paper was posted on 2026-03-03, only ~10 weeks before this review. The lower bound $M_{\mathbb{P}(n)}(n)=\Omega(n^3/\ln n)$ is established in the paper (Theorem 5); the upper bound that would nail the constant 1/6 is explicitly listed as open. arXiv:2605.13628 (Conlon, Fox, Pham) appeared in the search but concerns cap sets in finite fields and is unrelated. No other citing paper was found.
